Elements.Text: ● In the AddRoundKey step, we exclusive-or the result from previous steps with a set of keys derived from the 128-bit secret key

● The operation of the AddRoundKey step, therefore, can be expressed as follows:
[don, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, d

$$ \begin{array}{c} d_{0,0} \quad d_{0,1} \quad d_{0,2} \quad d_{0,3} \\ d_{1,0} \quad d_{1,1} \quad d_{1,2} \quad d_{1,3} \\ d_{2,0} \quad d_{2,1} \quad d_{2,2} \quad d_{2,3} \\ d_{3,0} \quad d_{3,1} \quad d_{3,2} \quad d_{3,3} \end{array} \quad \oplus \quad \begin{array}{c} k_{0,0} \quad k_{0,1} \quad k_{0,2} \quad k_{0,3} \\ k_{1,0} \quad k_{1,1} \quad k_{1,2} \quad k_{1,3} \\ k_{2,0} \quad k_{2,1} \quad k_{2,2} \quad k_{2,3} \\ k_{3,0} \quad k_{3,1} \quad k_{3,2} \quad k_{3,3} \end{array} \\ = \begin{array}{c} e_{0,0} \quad e_{0,1} \quad e_{0,2} \quad e_{0,3} \\ e_{1,0} \quad e_{1,1} \quad e_{1,2} \quad e_{1,3} \\ e_{2,0} \quad e_{2,1} \quad e_{2,2} \quad e_{2,3} \\ e_{3,0} \quad e_{3,1} \quad e_{3,2} \quad e_{3,3} \end{array} \end{array} $$
● The critical part of performing this step is determining how the matrix of keys, $k_{i,j}$, for this round, are derived from the single 128-bit secret key, $K$
